why: The F-15 economics block is OPTIONAL and every sub-field is optional; an empty object is valid (no constraints, no costs). Pins myelin src/economics.test.ts:29-32.

Documents the COMPLETE current shape the reference validator accepts (economics.test.ts:34-51). This is the whole of what the wire specifies today; every semantic (unit, aggregation, enforcement) is OPEN (RFC §5). The block round-trips verbatim — it is never normalized.
max_cost_usd is `>= 0`, so 0 is valid (a zero-budget declaration). Lower-boundary vector; contrast max_tokens which is `>= 1`. Pins economics.test.ts:71-73.

billing_ref accepts up to and including 256 characters; 257 is rejected (see invalid.json companion). Upper-boundary vector; economics.test.ts:143-146. billing_ref is the ONLY length-bounded economics field.

MASKING case. A reader may ASSUME total_tokens = input+output is enforced; it is NOT (validator comment: hubs aggregate non-arithmetically). 100 != 15 validates. This coincidental acceptance masks the dual-token / no-reconciliation gap (RFC §5.3; economics.test.ts:116-120).

COLLISION of meaning. The cost fields are named `_usd`, yet `currency` declares CHF; both validate and the wire cannot say whether 5 and 3 are USD or CHF. Two conforming implementations may bill in different currencies from the same bytes. Unresolved (RFC §5.2).

input.injected_by_relay: arbitrary annotation an intermediary added; the field is unknown, unsigned, and unbounded

value.injected_by_relay: arbitrary annotation an intermediary added; the field is unknown, unsigned, and unbounded

why: SECURITY. economics is additionalProperties:true at every level with NO maxProperties/maxLength AND is excluded from SIGNABLE_FIELDS (canonicalize.ts:24-26): any relay can inject arbitrary, arbitrarily-large content without invalidating a stamp. Documents the unauthenticated, unbounded, mutable channel (RFC §4, §7.2).
model-id must start with a letter; the real id `gpt-4o` passes. Contrast the invalid.json companion `4o-mini`, which starts with a digit and is REJECTED. Edge of MODEL_ID_RE (RFC §2.3, Appendix A).

MODEL_ID_RE `^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$` has NO -(?!-) lookahead, so `--` is permitted inside a model id — unlike capability tags and the did:mf method-specific-id, which forbid it. Documents the alphabet divergence (RFC §2.3, Appendix A).

wallet is the did:mf DID of RFC-0001, unchanged. A wallet value can be byte-identical to a principal/stack/agent/service/hub DID (flat-namespace class collision), so 'who is paid' rests on convention, not on the grammar. Blocked on cortex#1880 (RFC §5.6, RFC-0001).
